summary: Investigation confirms industrial espionage involving Donald Norby, who exfiltrated confidential SwiftLogic Inc. documents. Malware on the target device facilitated periodic PDF exfiltration to external IP 50.56.29.109. Negotiation of intellectual property sale was identified via SMS/email with partner Mr. E. Yob. Suspect is deceased. Coverage: Filesystem=YES; Registry=NO; EventLogs=NO;
null_hypothesis: The suspect was a legitimate employee using the device for authorized data transfer, and the external IP was a corporate cloud storage endpoint. This is refuted by the presence of hardcoded malware references to the IP and the context of unauthorized negotiation of intellectual property sale with Mr. E. Yob.

artifacts.name: External IP
artifacts.description: External server identified in malware code for data exfiltration.
artifacts.recommended_validation: Check firewall logs for connection duration and volume.

artifacts.name: Malware Exfiltration Mechanism
artifacts.value: SMS from malware confirming periodic exfiltration of PDFs
artifacts.description: SMS logs indicate automated exfiltration of confidential PDFs and negotiation of intellectual property sale with Mr. E. Yob.
artifacts.recommended_validation: Analyze SMS logs for timestamp correlation with file access.

artifacts.name: Confidential Documents
artifacts.value: Confidential competitor documents on phone SD card
artifacts.description: Unauthorized storage of sensitive SwiftLogic Inc. documents on mobile device.
artifacts.recommended_validation: Verify file hashes against known corporate document repository.
